what_it_does: Defines window.CV_PICK, an IIFE document-wide element picker. When started, it spotlights the element under the cursor with a fixed overlay box and a role·tag label (hover), and on click captures a structured descriptor of that element (role, tag, id, classes, text, size, layout, box, child roles, computed styles) plus a live node reference, then invokes the supplied callback. Esc cancels. The app's own chrome (Vi panel/fab, command bar, top bar, toast) is excluded from picking.
observations: Overlay box uses a huge box-shadow (0 0 0 9999px rgba) to dim everything outside the spotlighted element
z-index uses max int values (2147483646/47) so overlays sit above all app chrome
roleOf() heuristically classifies an element by tag + className regex into button/heading/card/image/badge/value/link/field/icon/navigation/element
excluded() uses el.closest against a selector list of app chrome to avoid picking the picker's own UI
describe() trims and caps every captured field (text 120 chars, classes 6, children 8, shadow 60, etc.) to keep the descriptor compact for Vi
clsOf() handles SVG className (baseVal) vs string className
uses elementFromPoint on mousemove to track the hovered element, capturing in capture phase (true)
layout/box/styles selectively omit default values (e.g. flexDirection only if not 'row', margin only if not '0px')
standouts: Captures a compact, Vi-readable structured descriptor (role + layout + computed styles + child roles) rather than raw DOM, purpose-built for the AI to reason about and restyle
Works on ANY real element on screen, not only app-rendered ones
conventions: IIFE + window global registry pattern shared across the atomicity layer
Token-aware overlay styling via var(--accent-gold), var(--font-mono) fallbacks
Capture-phase listeners with preventDefault/stopPropagation to intercept the click before the app handles it
Self-excludes its own chrome — a clean boundary between tool and target
concerns: roleOf() classification is regex/heuristic over class names and can misclassify against unconventional markup
Descriptor field caps are hardcoded magic numbers scattered through describe()
Overlay nodes are appended once and never removed (only hidden), persisting in the DOM
notes: The element picker: arm it and any element you hover gets spotlighted+labelled, and a click hands Vi a compact structured descriptor (role, layout, computed styles, children) plus a live reference for in-place restyling.
questions: Who consumes the descriptor callback — is it the mode-engine's inspect mode and/or ATOMICITY.pickElement?
How does the roleOf heuristic stay in sync with the design system's actual component/atom roles?
purpose_vs_actual: Match — docstring describes a document-wide inspect-and-capture picker handing structured context to Vi; the code implements hover spotlight, click capture, descriptor build, and chrome exclusion exactly as stated.
intention_signals: Tries to turn any on-screen element into addressable, AI-readable context — a bridge from raw DOM to a descriptor Vi can talk about and build/restyle from, while never picking the tool's own UI.
novelty: The role-inference heuristic combined with a deliberately compact, default-eliding computed-style descriptor designed for an LLM to consume is the distinctive element.
contribution: Contributes the element-capture front end of the inspect flow: a universal hover-spotlight picker that converts any DOM element into a compact structured descriptor (role/layout/styles/children) plus a live node reference for Vi to reason about and for the override layer to restyle.
summary_for_embedding: A JS IIFE (window.CV_PICK) document-wide element picker that hover-spotlights elements and on click captures a compact structured descriptor (role, layout, computed styles, child roles) plus a live node reference for Vi, excluding the app's own chrome.
intention_for_embedding: To convert any on-screen element into AI-readable structured context plus a live reference so Vi can discuss and restyle it in place.
